rag_rules: Place input range assumptions in component type annexes using visible feature names and concrete bounds.
Use AGREE syntax for assumptions and guarantees with valid component scope and type alignment.
generic_rules: Use visible identifiers from the AADL model to ensure AGREE expressions are syntactically valid.
Declare auxiliary variables with explicit types to avoid type mismatch diagnostics.
Avoid referencing AADL properties directly in AGREE expressions unless they are exposed as typed features.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees must be placed in valid component type or implementation scopes.
anti_patterns: Do not reference Boolean ports as numeric values in range comparisons.
Do not assume AADL property names are automatically available as AGREE variables.
irrelevant_topics: AADL property associations and timing parameters
Implementation-level assign statements in type scopes
Model fusion and owner/scope preservation rules
